title: cereus leucanthus
intro: Cereus leucanthus, also known as the Night-Blooming Cereus, is a species of cactus that is native to Mexico and is characterized by its white flowers that bloom at night and its ability to grow up to 6 meters tall. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, and has been used to treat various health issues such as wounds, fever, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Cereus leucanthus include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The plant is typically prepared as a tea or tincture, and can also be dried and used as a powder or infused into oil for topical use.
